apply: Apply the concepts of accounting to prepare financial statements (Balance Sheet, Income Statement) for a given set of transactions.
Prepare and analyze the financial statements of a company to determine its financial position and performance.
Apply the accounting standards and policies to prepare financial statements.
analyze: Analyze the financial statements to evaluate a company's financial performance and position.
Compare and contrast the financial statements of two or more companies to identify trends and areas of improvement.
Analyze the impact of various financial transactions on the financial statements of a company.
remember: Recall the format and layout of financial statements (Balance Sheet, Income Statement) as per the Companies Act.
Identify and explain the various components of financial statements (Assets, Liabilities, Equity, Revenues, Expenses).
Explain the principles of accounting and their application in financial statements.
understand: Understand the concept of financial position and financial performance as reflected in the Balance Sheet and Income Statement.
Explain the difference between Accounting Equation and Accounting Concept.
Describe the accounting treatment of various financial transactions in financial statements.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT, the objectives of financial statements are to provide a true and fair view of a company's financial position and performance.
board_exam_objectives: To test the candidate's ability to prepare and analyze financial statements (Balance Sheet, Income Statement) as per the Companies Act and accounting standards.
To assess the candidate's understanding of the principles of accounting and their application in financial statements.
